AcceptLink.name: AcceptLink
AcceptLink.documentation:
Accepts a link request between gateways.

When a requester gateway requests to link with a responder gateway, the responder can use this operation to accept the link request and establish the connection.

AssociateCertificate.name: AssociateCertificate
AssociateCertificate.documentation: Associates an ACM certificate with a responder gateway.

CreateLink.name: CreateLink
CreateLink.documentation:
Creates a new link between gateways.

Establishes a connection that allows gateways to communicate and exchange bid requests and responses.

CreateLinkRoutingRule.name: CreateLinkRoutingRule
CreateLinkRoutingRule.documentation:
Creates a routing rule for a link.

Routing rules use priority-based evaluation where lower priority numbers are evaluated first. Each rule specifies conditions that must all match for the rule to apply.

CreateResponderGateway.name: CreateResponderGateway
CreateResponderGateway.documentation:
Creates a responder gateway.

A domain name or managed endpoint is required.

DeleteLink.name: DeleteLink
DeleteLink.documentation:
Deletes a link between gateways.

Permanently removes the connection between gateways. This action cannot be undone.

DeleteLinkRoutingRule.name: DeleteLinkRoutingRule
DeleteLinkRoutingRule.documentation: Deletes a routing rule from a link.

DisassociateCertificate.name: DisassociateCertificate
DisassociateCertificate.documentation: Removes a certificate association from a responder gateway.

GetCertificateAssociation.name: GetCertificateAssociation
GetCertificateAssociation.documentation: Retrieves the details of a certificate association with a responder gateway.

GetInboundExternalLink.name: GetInboundExternalLink
GetInboundExternalLink.documentation: Retrieves information about an inbound external link.

GetLink.name: GetLink
GetLink.documentation:
Retrieves information about a link between gateways.

Returns detailed information about the link configuration, status, and associated gateways.

GetLinkRoutingRule.name: GetLinkRoutingRule
GetLinkRoutingRule.documentation: Retrieves the details of a routing rule for a link.

GetOutboundExternalLink.name: GetOutboundExternalLink
GetOutboundExternalLink.documentation: Retrieves information about an outbound external link.

ListCertificateAssociations.name: ListCertificateAssociations
ListCertificateAssociations.documentation: Lists the certificate associations for a responder gateway.

ListLinkRoutingRules.name: ListLinkRoutingRules
ListLinkRoutingRules.documentation: Lists the routing rules for a link.

ListLinks.name: ListLinks
ListLinks.documentation:
Lists links associated with gateways.

Returns a list of all links for the specified gateways, including their status and configuration details.

RejectLink.name: RejectLink
RejectLink.documentation:
Rejects a link request between gateways.

When a requester gateway requests to link with a responder gateway, the responder can use this operation to decline the link request.

TagResource.name: TagResource
TagResource.documentation: Assigns one or more tags (key-value pairs) to the specified resource.

UntagResource.name: UntagResource
UntagResource.documentation: Removes a tag or tags from a resource.

UpdateLink.name: UpdateLink
UpdateLink.documentation:
Updates the configuration of a link between gateways.

Allows you to modify settings and parameters for an existing link.

UpdateLinkRoutingRule.name: UpdateLinkRoutingRule
UpdateLinkRoutingRule.documentation: Updates a routing rule for a link.

clientToken.documentation:
Specifies a unique, case-sensitive identifier that you provide to ensure the idempotency of the request. This lets you safely retry the request without accidentally performing the same operation a second time. Passing the same value to a later call to an operation requires that you also pass the same value for all other parameters. We recommend that you use a UUID type of value.

If you don't provide this value, then Amazon Web Services generates a random one for you.

If you retry the operation with the same ClientToken, but with different parameters, the retry fails with an IdempotentParameterMismatch error.

port.documentation: The port to use for health check probes. Valid range is 80 to 65535.

path.documentation: The destination path for the health check request. Must start with /.

protocol.documentation: The protocol to use for health check probes.

timeoutMs.documentation: The timeout for each health check probe, in milliseconds. Valid range is 100 to 5000.

intervalSeconds.documentation: The interval between health check probes, in seconds. Valid range is 5 to 60.

statusCodeMatcher.documentation: The expected HTTP status code or status code pattern from healthy instances. Supports a single code (for example, 200), a range (for example, 200-299), or a comma-separated list (for example, 200,204).

healthyThresholdCount.documentation: The number of consecutive successful health checks required before an instance is considered healthy. Valid range is 2 to 10.

unhealthyThresholdCount.documentation: The number of consecutive failed health checks required before an instance is considered unhealthy. Valid range is 2 to 10.

HealthCheckConfig.documentation: The health check configuration for a managed endpoint. Defines how the service probes instances in the Auto Scaling group to determine their health status.

Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page. Keep all other arguments unchanged. Each pagination token expires after 24 hours. Using an expired pagination token will return an HTTP 400 InvalidToken error.

pathPrefix.documentation: Path prefix matching — strict starts-with, no wildcard (preferred for new rules). Must start with /; RFC 3986 unreserved plus /

pathExact.documentation: Exact path match — must start with /; RFC 3986 unreserved plus /

queryStringExists.documentation: Query string key presence check (any value accepted) — RFC 3986 unreserved characters

RuleCondition.documentation: Conditions bag for a routing rule. All non-null fields must match (AND logic). At least one field must be set (enforced by CP).

RulePriority.documentation: WAF-style evaluation priority. Lower number = evaluated first (priority 1 before 10). Gaps are allowed (1, 10, 20 is valid). Must be between 1 and 1000 inclusive. Uniqueness per link among non-deleted rules is enforced at the API layer (HTTP 409 on conflict).

ServiceQuotaExceededException.documentation: The request could not be completed because you exceeded a service quota.

tags.documentation: A map of the key-value pairs of the tag or tags to assign to the resource.

priority.documentation: The updated priority of the routing rule. Lower numbers are evaluated first. Valid values are 1 to 1000. Priority must be unique among non-deleted rules within a link.

conditions.documentation: The updated conditions for the routing rule. All specified fields must match for the rule to apply. At least one condition field must be set.

documentation:
Amazon Web Services RTB Fabric provides secure, low-latency infrastructure for connecting real-time bidding (RTB) applications. Rather than hosting applications directly, RTB Fabric acts as the connecting fabric that enables your applications to communicate efficiently over private networks instead of the public internet. You maintain complete control over your applications, data, and bidding decisions, while RTB Fabric provides the underlying infrastructure for secure, reliable connectivity.

You can use these APIs to complete RTB Fabric tasks, such as setting up audit log ingestions or viewing user access. For more information about RTB Fabric, including the required permissions to use the service, see the Amazon Web Services RTB Fabric User Guide.
